Registers bulk I/O handlers for different domains in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code registers handlers for different bulk I/O domains using the `REGISTER_BULKIO_HANDLER` macro. Each handler is associated with a specific domain type and a unique domain ID, which must remain constant. The domains include `Memory`, `ExternalFlash`, `Framebuffer`, and `Coredump`, with an additional `PFS` domain registered conditionally if `RECOVERY_FW` is not defined. The `vtable` parameter in the macro refers to a `PulseBulkIODomainHandler` for each domain, which likely defines the operations or behaviors for handling I/O in that domain. This code is part of a configuration or initialization process for setting up domain-specific I/O handlers.
